inventory_reconciliation.reconciliation_notes: P1 and P2 are devoid of visible vegetative matter. P3 and P4 appear to be the same plant or cuttings of the same species.

plant_audit.p1: Systemic Loss. Pot is empty; soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.p2: Systemic Loss. Pot is empty; soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.p3: Stable. Pothos leaf remains turgid. Rabbit anchor present.
plant_audit.p4: Stable. Single leaf cutting remains green, though posture is slightly drooping.

biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P4. Electronic component (IC chip) introduced to desk surface at T-3.

temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: New intervention: Electronic component placed on desk surface.

visual_health_inference.p3: Healthy. Leaf color is vibrant green, no chlorosis.
visual_health_inference.p4: Fair. Cutting is alive but lacks root establishment visible on surface.

anomalies: Presence of an integrated circuit (IC) on the desk surface starting at T-3.
Eggshell fragments used as soil top-dressing in P4.
Total absence of P1 (String of Nickels) and P2 (Mexican Mint) specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of stasis. The primary biological activity is restricted to the Pothos specimens in P3 and P4. The yellow pot (P1) and the secondary black pot (P2) are effectively barren, suggesting a failure of the original specimens. The introduction of an electronic component at T-3 suggests an external, non-botanical intervention in the workspace. The Pothos in P3 remains the most robust element of the collection.
